title: Plimsoll policy
description: Schema for a Plimsoll policy file: the declarative budget and trajectory rules used to evaluate agent traces. All properties are optional; an empty object is a valid policy that applies built-in defaults.

$schema.description: Optional reference to this JSON Schema, allowing editors to provide validation and autocomplete.

version.description: Optional free-form version label for the policy document (ignored by the loader).

allowed_tools.description: Allowlist of tool names; if non-empty, only these tools may appear in a trace.

forbidden_tools.description: Denylist of tool names that must never appear in a trace.

required_tools.description: Tool names that must appear at least once in a trace.

max_steps.description: Maximum number of agent steps (spans) permitted in a trace.

max_duration_ms.description: Maximum total wall-clock duration of a trace, in milliseconds.

max_tokens.description: Maximum total token count permitted across a trace.

max_estimated_cost_usd.description: Maximum estimated total cost of a trace, in US dollars.

max_repeated_action_count.description: Maximum number of times an identical action may repeat before it is flagged (defaults to 1).

pii_patterns.description: Regular expressions whose matches in trace content are flagged as personally identifiable information.

secret_patterns.description: Regular expressions whose matches in trace content are flagged as leaked secrets.

expected_output_mode.description: How the final output is compared to the expected output: substring containment or exact match (defaults to 'contains').

max_tool_sequence_distance.description: Maximum allowed edit distance between the observed tool sequence and the expected one.

trajectory_match_mode.description: How the observed trajectory is matched against the expected trajectory.

must_precede.description: Ordering invariants: for each pair, any run that performs 'after' must have performed 'before' at an earlier step (e.g. require approvals before a privileged action). A run that never performs 'after' is valid.

after.description: Tool that must not occur before 'before'.
